Items.Key: command injection via admin exec command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|authenticated admins (or attackers who gained admin access) can execute arbitrary system commands with full process privileges.|admin command starting with 'exec ' followed by any system command|remove the exec command or implement a strict whitelist of allowed commands. never pass user input directly to system().->command injection via admin exec command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|authenticated admins (or attackers who gained admin access) can execute arbitrary system commands with full process privileges.|admin command starting with 'exec ' followed by any system command|remove the exec command or implement a strict whitelist of allowed commands. never pass user input directly to system().

command injection in emergency override audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|#ifdef _win32 const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; #else const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; #endif system(command.c_str());|arbitrary command execution when emergency override is triggered. the audit_path can be manipulated via environment variables (temp/tmpdir).|emergency override authentication (after 5 failed login attempts) with manipulated temp or tmpdir environment variables|use direct file i/o (e.g., std::ofstream) instead of system() calls for logging. sanitize and validate all paths.->command injection in emergency override audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|#ifdef _win32 const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; #else const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; #endif system(command.c_str());|arbitrary command execution when emergency override is triggered. the audit_path can be manipulated via environment variables (temp/tmpdir).|emergency override authentication (after 5 failed login attempts) with manipulated temp or tmpdir environment variables|use direct file i/o (e.g., std::ofstream) instead of system() calls for logging. sanitize and validate all paths.

user-controlled log format enables format string attacks|format string vulnerability|cwe-134|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|208|215|string_utils::runtime_log_format, string_utils::set_runtime_log_format|inline std::string& runtime_log_format() { static std::string active_format = config::security::log_format; return active_format; } inline void set_runtime_log_format(const std::string& fmt) { if (!fmt.empty()) runtime_log_format() = fmt;|the log format string is user-controllable (via config file or direct call to set_runtime_log_format) and used as a format string in printf, enabling format string attacks.|setting log format to a string containing format specifiers (e.g., via config file or set_runtime_log_format)|remove the ability to modify the log format at runtime. use a fixed, safe format string for all logging.->user-controlled log format enables format string attacks|format string vulnerability|cwe-134|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|208|215|string_utils::runtime_log_format, string_utils::set_runtime_log_format|inline std::string& runtime_log_format() { static std::string active_format = config::security::log_format; return active_format; } inline void set_runtime_log_format(const std::string& fmt) { if (!fmt.empty()) runtime_log_format() = fmt;|the log format string is user-controllable (via config file or direct call to set_runtime_log_format) and used as a format string in printf, enabling format string attacks.|setting log format to a string containing format specifiers (e.g., via config file or set_runtime_log_format)|remove the ability to modify the log format at runtime. use a fixed, safe format string for all logging.

buffer overflow in evaluate_expression for long inputs|buffer overflow|cwe-125|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_));|when expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_), strcpy is used without bounds checking, leading to buffer overflow. the input_buffer_ size is config::limits::stack_buffer_size (1024 bytes).|evaluating an expression with length >= 1024 bytes|always use safe_string_copy with sizeof(input_buffer_) as the destination size, regardless of the input length. remove the unsafe strcpy branch.->buffer overflow in evaluate_expression for long inputs|buffer overflow|cwe-125|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_));|when expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_), strcpy is used without bounds checking, leading to buffer overflow. the input_buffer_ size is config::limits::stack_buffer_size (1024 bytes).|evaluating an expression with length >= 1024 bytes|always use safe_string_copy with sizeof(input_buffer_) as the destination size, regardless of the input length. remove the unsafe strcpy branch.

denial of service via division by zero in evaluate|denial of service|cwe-369|medium|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|461|465|parser::expressionevaluator::evaluate|case math_engine::tokentype::operator_div: { double right_val = evaluate(node->right); if (right_val == 0.0) return infinity; return evaluate(node->left) / right_val;|division by zero returns infinity, which may cause undefined behavior in subsequent calculations or when the result is used in other contexts (e.g., formatting).|evaluating an expression with a division by zero (e.g., '1/0')|throw an exception or return a special error value (e.g., nan) instead of infinity, and handle it appropriately in the caller.->denial of service via division by zero in evaluate|denial of service|cwe-369|medium|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|461|465|parser::expressionevaluator::evaluate|case math_engine::tokentype::operator_div: { double right_val = evaluate(node->right); if (right_val == 0.0) return infinity; return evaluate(node->left) / right_val;|division by zero returns infinity, which may cause undefined behavior in subsequent calculations or when the result is used in other contexts (e.g., formatting).|evaluating an expression with a division by zero (e.g., '1/0')|throw an exception or return a special error value (e.g., nan) instead of infinity, and handle it appropriately in the caller.

denial of service via infinite loop in factorial function|denial of service|cwe-835|medium|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|312|320|math_engine::functionregistry::functionregistry (fact function)|unary_functions_["fact"] = [](double x) -> double { if (x < 0) return nan; if (x == 0 || x == 1) return 1; long long result = 1; for (long long i = 2; i <= static_cast<long long>(x); i++) { result *= i;|for very large x (e.g., 1e100), the loop condition i <= static_cast<long long>(x) may cause an extremely long or infinite loop due to integer overflow or impractical iteration count.|calling fact() with a very large argument (e.g., fact(1e100))|add a maximum input check (e.g., x > 20) and return an error or infinity for values that are too large.->denial of service via infinite loop in factorial function|denial of service|cwe-835|medium|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|312|320|math_engine::functionregistry::functionregistry (fact function)|unary_functions_["fact"] = [](double x) -> double { if (x < 0) return nan; if (x == 0 || x == 1) return 1; long long result = 1; for (long long i = 2; i <= static_cast<long long>(x); i++) { result *= i;|for very large x (e.g., 1e100), the loop condition i <= static_cast<long long>(x) may cause an extremely long or infinite loop due to integer overflow or impractical iteration count.|calling fact() with a very large argument (e.g., fact(1e100))|add a maximum input check (e.g., x > 20) and return an error or infinity for values that are too large.

insufficient input validation in evaluate_expression|missing input validation|cwe-20|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|879|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|double evaluate_expression(const std::string& expression) { if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); } string_utils::log_debug_message(expression.c_str()); schedule_expression_analysis(expression); try { if (expression.find('+') != std::string::npos) { ... }|the function does not validate the length of the input expression against config::limits::max_expression_length (2048 bytes). this can lead to buffer overflows and other issues in downstream processing.|passing an expression longer than 2048 bytes to evaluate_expression|add a check at the beginning of the function: if (expression.length() > config::limits::max_expression_length) throw std::invalid_argument("expression too long");->insufficient input validation in evaluate_expression|missing input validation|cwe-20|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|830|879|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|double evaluate_expression(const std::string& expression) { if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); } string_utils::log_debug_message(expression.c_str()); schedule_expression_analysis(expression); try { if (expression.find('+') != std::string::npos) { ... }|the function does not validate the length of the input expression against config::limits::max_expression_length (2048 bytes). this can lead to buffer overflows and other issues in downstream processing.|passing an expression longer than 2048 bytes to evaluate_expression|add a check at the beginning of the function: if (expression.length() > config::limits::max_expression_length) throw std::invalid_argument("expression too long");

unsafe use of std::strtod without end pointer validation|improper input validation|cwe-131|medium|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|762|769|calculator::supercalc::tokenize_expression_for_parser|const char* start = expression.c_str() + i; char* end = nullptr; double value = std::strtod(start, &end); size_t len = static_cast<size_t>(end - start); if (len == 0) { ++i; continue; }|if strtod fails to parse a number, end may equal start, but the code only checks if len == 0. however, if end is nullptr (unlikely but possible), this would cause undefined behavior when computing len.|tokenizing an expression with malformed numeric literals|add a check for end == nullptr: if (end == nullptr || len == 0) { ++i; continue; }->unsafe use of std::strtod without end pointer validation|improper input validation|cwe-131|medium|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|762|769|calculator::supercalc::tokenize_expression_for_parser|const char* start = expression.c_str() + i; char* end = nullptr; double value = std::strtod(start, &end); size_t len = static_cast<size_t>(end - start); if (len == 0) { ++i; continue; }|if strtod fails to parse a number, end may equal start, but the code only checks if len == 0. however, if end is nullptr (unlikely but possible), this would cause undefined behavior when computing len.|tokenizing an expression with malformed numeric literals|add a check for end == nullptr: if (end == nullptr || len == 0) { ++i; continue; }

potential null pointer dereference in memorypool::deallocate|null pointer dereference|cwe-476|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|160|170|memory::memorypool::deallocate|void deallocate(void* ptr) { std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(pool_mutex_); for (auto& block : blocks_) { if (block.data == ptr) { if (block.ref_count.fetch_sub(1, std::memory_order_relaxed) == 1) { block.in_use = false; } return;|if ptr is nullptr, the loop may not find a matching block, but the function does not explicitly handle nullptr. however, the main issue is that if ptr is not found, the function silently returns without freeing memory, which could indicate a double-free or invalid pointer.|calling deallocate with a pointer not managed by the pool or nullptr|add a check at the beginning: if (ptr == nullptr) return; and consider logging or throwing an exception if the pointer is not found in the pool.->potential null pointer dereference in memorypool::deallocate|null pointer dereference|cwe-476|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|160|170|memory::memorypool::deallocate|void deallocate(void* ptr) { std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(pool_mutex_); for (auto& block : blocks_) { if (block.data == ptr) { if (block.ref_count.fetch_sub(1, std::memory_order_relaxed) == 1) { block.in_use = false; } return;|if ptr is nullptr, the loop may not find a matching block, but the function does not explicitly handle nullptr. however, the main issue is that if ptr is not found, the function silently returns without freeing memory, which could indicate a double-free or invalid pointer.|calling deallocate with a pointer not managed by the pool or nullptr|add a check at the beginning: if (ptr == nullptr) return; and consider logging or throwing an exception if the pointer is not found in the pool.

unused return value from snprintf in format_string|improper error handling|cwe-252|low|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|string_utils::format_string|template<typename... args> inline std::string format_string(const char* fmt, args... args) { constexpr size_t buf_size = 1024; char buffer[buf_size]; snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...); return buffer;|the return value of snprintf (which indicates the number of bytes written or needed) is ignored. if the formatted string exceeds buf_size, the result will be truncated, and the caller will not be aware of the truncation.|calling format_string with a format string and arguments that produce output >= 1024 bytes|check the return value of snprintf and handle truncation (e.g., by throwing an exception or using a dynamically sized buffer).->unused return value from snprintf in format_string|improper error handling|cwe-252|low|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|string_utils::format_string|template<typename... args> inline std::string format_string(const char* fmt, args... args) { constexpr size_t buf_size = 1024; char buffer[buf_size]; snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...); return buffer;|the return value of snprintf (which indicates the number of bytes written or needed) is ignored. if the formatted string exceeds buf_size, the result will be truncated, and the caller will not be aware of the truncation.|calling format_string with a format string and arguments that produce output >= 1024 bytes|check the return value of snprintf and handle truncation (e.g., by throwing an exception or using a dynamically sized buffer).

buffer overflow in expressionparser constructor|buffer overflow|cwe-125|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|366|368|parser::expressionparser::expressionparser|buffer_size_ = 256; expression_buffer_ = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(buffer_size_));|the buffer is allocated with size 256, but later used with strcpy (line 376) which does not check bounds. long expressions can overflow the buffer.|parsing an expression whose string representation exceeds 255 bytes (256 including null terminator)|use a dynamically sized buffer or ensure the buffer is large enough for the maximum expected expression length (config::limits::max_expression_length). use safe_string_copy instead of strcpy.->buffer overflow via strcpy in expressionparser::parse|buffer overflow|cwe-125|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|374|376|parser::expressionparser::parse|std::string expr_str; for (const auto& token : tokens) expr_str += token.text + " "; if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());|expr_str can grow arbitrarily large (up to the sum of all token texts + spaces), and strcpy will overflow the fixed-size expression_buffer_ (256 bytes).|parsing an expression with tokens whose combined length exceeds 255 bytes|replace strcpy with a bounds-checked copy (e.g., safe_string_copy) and ensure the buffer is large enough. alternatively, use std::string for expression_buffer_.

missing bounds check in safe_string_copy for dest_size == 1|buffer overflow|cwe-125|low|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|200|206|string_utils::safe_string_copy|inline void safe_string_copy(char* dest, const char* src, size_t dest_size) { if (!dest || !src || dest_size == 0) return; size_t src_len = strlen(src); if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|when dest_size == 1, the function copies 1 byte from src (if src is non-empty) and then writes a null terminator at dest[1], which is out of bounds. this is a special case of the earlier buffer overflow finding.|calling safe_string_copy with dest_size == 1 and a non-empty source string|change the condition to if (src_len >= dest_size) src_len = dest_size - 1; to ensure space for the null terminator in all cases.

potential integer overflow in memorypool::allocate|integer overflow|cwe-190|low|0,7|enhanced_calc.cpp|132|134|memory::memorypool::allocate|constexpr size_t split_metadata_bytes = 64; size_t remaining = previous_size - size - split_metadata_bytes;|if size is very large (close to size_max), the subtraction previous_size - size - split_metadata_bytes could underflow, leading to a very large remaining value and incorrect memory management.|allocating a block with size close to size_max|add a check to ensure that size + split_metadata_bytes <= previous_size before performing the subtraction.

truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 against the now-visible true vulnerabilities in enhanced_calc.cpp. Strictly evaluates whether Run 2's findings match the ground truth, with exact quote evidence.

Items.Notes: Run 2 explicitly identified the format string vulnerability via printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input) in log_debug_message, matching SC-V3-001's anchors (log_format, runtime_log_format, printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input)).
Run 2 correctly identified the integer overflow in the factorial computation (result *= i), matching SC-V3-002's anchors (result *= i; fact).

Run 2 reported a memory leak in stage_deferred_history_flush (SC-V3-003's location) but did not identify the use-after-free. The memory leak finding does not cover the use-after-free vulnerability (cleanup with scratch buffers not freed before use).
Run 2 explicitly identified command injection via system() in config validation, matching SC-V3-004's anchors (validator_cmd + " " + config_file; system(command.c_str()); admin validate).
Run 2 identified the stack buffer overflow via strcpy in evaluate_expression for long inputs, matching SC-V3-005's anchors (strcpy(input_buffer_; dest[src_len]; src_len > dest_size).

Run 2 correctly identified the race condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment due to reading value_ outside the lock, matching SC-V3-006's anchors (volatile long long; old_value = value_; lock_guard; value_ = old_value + 1).

Run 2 identified the logic bomb (emergency override) in authentication, matching SC-V3-007's anchors (login_attempts_ > 5; EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE; authenticated_ = true). Note: CWE-287 vs CWE-511 is a classification difference, but the vulnerability is correctly identified.
Run 2 did not identify path traversal in configuration loading (config_dir_ + filename; load_config(filename)).

Run 2 identified the heap overflow in the expression parser due to unbounded strcpy into a fixed-size buffer, matching SC-V3-009's anchors (buffer_size_ = 256; strcpy(expression_buffer_; schedule_expression_analysis).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: {"title": "Hardcoded administrative password in plaintext", ... "evidence": "constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = \"SC_ENT_2025_AUTH\";"}
Items.Notes: Run 2 explicitly identified the hardcoded admin credentials, matching SC-V3-010's anchors (ADMIN_SECRET; SC_ENT_2025_AUTH).
Run 2 did not identify the weak PRNG for session tokens (std::mt19937; uniform_int_distribution; 1000, 9999).
Run 2 did not identify the thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging (static std::tm cached_time; localtime; cached_time = *local_time).
Run 2 identified insecure temporary file handling in append_override_audit_event, matching SC-V3-013's anchors (TEMP; TMPDIR; supercalc_emergency.log; system(command.c_str())).
Run 2 did not identify persistent authentication state (authenticated_; login_attempts_; session_token_).
Run 2 did not identify the unhandled exception in variable parsing (std::stod(tokens[3]); handle_variable_command).
Run 2 reported a memory leak in MemoryPool::allocate due to fragment handling but did not identify the integer underflow in block split (previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES).
Run 2 did not identify unbounded recursion in the parser (parse_expression; parse_factor; PAREN_OPEN).
Run 2 did not identify the data race in result cache (result_cache_; evaluate_cached; cache_mutex_; thread_pool_->enqueue).
Run 2 did not identify the TOCTOU in config loading (std::filesystem::exists; sleep_for; std::ifstream file).
Run 2 did not identify the integer division by zero in command routing (calculate_route_score; token_length; 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length)).
